crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis:
The provided set of macros consists of attributes for multiple sheets (Sheet1, Sheet2, Sheet3, Sheet4, Sheet5) and a function in Module1 named GetURL. The function GetURL takes a range object as input and returns the address of the first hyperlink in that range.

Based on the analysis of the macros, there are no indications of malicious intent or behaviors present. The macros primarily contain standard attributes and a simple function to retrieve hyperlink addresses from ranges. There are no signs of obfuscation, suspicious function calls, attempts at persistence, registry manipulation, downloading or executing files, self-replication mechanisms, or any other indicators commonly associated with malware.

Therefore, the macros are rated as Benign.

The macro defines a function named "GetURL" which takes a Range object as input. Upon execution, this function retrieves and returns the URL address associated with the first hyperlink present within the provided range.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Access To Windows DPAPI Master Keys By Uncommon Applications
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects file access requests to the the Windows Data Protection API Master keys by an uncommon application.
This can be a sign of credential stealing. Example case would be usage of mimikatz "dpapi::masterkey" function

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious File Access to Browser Credential Storage
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects file access to browser credential storage paths by non-browser processes, which may indicate credential access attempts.
Adversaries may attempt to access browser credential storage to extract sensitive information such as usernames and passwords or cookies.
This behavior is often commonly observed in credential stealing malware.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: frack113, X__Junior (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems), Parth-FourCore

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: System File Execution Location Anomaly
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the execution of a Windows system binary that is usually located in the system folder from an uncommon location.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Patrick Bareiss, Anton Kutepov, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious DNS Query for IP Lookup Service APIs
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects DNS queries for IP lookup services such as "api.ipify.org" originating from a non browser process.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Startup Folder File Write
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: A General detection for files being created in the Windows startup directory. This could be an indicator of persistence.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Hidden Executable In NTFS Alternate Data Stream
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the creation of an ADS (Alternate Data Stream) that contains an executable by looking at a non-empty Imphash

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious Network Connection to IP Lookup Service APIs
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects external IP address lookups by non-browser processes via services such as "api.ipify.org". This could be indicative of potential post compromise internet test activity.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: CurrentVersion Autorun Keys Modification
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects modification of autostart extensibility point (ASEP) in registry.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Victor Sergeev, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, Gleb Sukhodolskiy, Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community, Tim Shelton, frack113 (split)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious Schtasks Schedule Type With High Privileges
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects scheduled task creations or modification to be run with high privileges on a suspicious schedule type

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Access To Windows DPAPI Master Keys By Uncommon Applications
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects file access requests to the the Windows Data Protection API Master keys by an uncommon application.
This can be a sign of credential stealing. Example case would be usage of mimikatz "dpapi::masterkey" function

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious File Access to Browser Credential Storage
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects file access to browser credential storage paths by non-browser processes, which may indicate credential access attempts.
Adversaries may attempt to access browser credential storage to extract sensitive information such as usernames and passwords or cookies.
This behavior is often commonly observed in credential stealing malware.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: frack113, X__Junior (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems), Parth-FourCore

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Scheduled Task Creation Via Schtasks.EXE
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the creation of scheduled tasks by user accounts via the "schtasks" utility.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: File Deletion Via Del
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects execution of the builtin "del"/"erase" commands in order to delete files.
Adversaries may delete files left behind by the actions of their intrusion activity.
Malware, tools, or other non-native files dropped or created on a system by an adversary may leave traces to indicate to what was done within a network and how.
Removal of these files can occur during an intrusion, or as part of a post-intrusion process to minimize the adversary's footprint.

android.permission.CHANGE_NETWORK_STATE.full_description: Allows the app to change the state of network connectivity.

com.android.launcher.permission.UNINSTAL.full_description:
Allows the application to remove
        Homescreen shortcuts without user intervention.

android.permission.READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE.short_description: read the contents of your SD card
android.permission.READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE.full_description: Allows the app to read the contents of your SD card.

android.permission.CAMERA.full_description: This app can take pictures and record videos using the camera at any time.

android.permission.INTERNET.full_description:
Allows the app to create
     network sockets and use custom network protocols. The browser and other
     applications provide means to send data to the internet, so this
     permission is not required to send data to the internet.

android.permission.REQUEST_INSTALL_PACKA.full_description: Allows an application to request installation of packages.

com.android.launcher.permission.INSTALL_.full_description:
Allows an application to add
        Homescreen shortcuts without user intervention.

android.permission.ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE.full_description:
Allows the app to view
      information about network connections such as which networks exist and are
      connected.

android.permission.GET_TASKS.full_description:
Allows the app to retrieve information
       about currently and recently running tasks.  This may allow the app to
       discover information about which applications are used on the device.

android.permission.WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAG.short_description: modify or delete the contents of your SD card
android.permission.WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAG.full_description: Allows the app to write to the SD card.

android.permission.BROADCAST_STICKY.full_description:
Allows the app to
    send sticky broadcasts, which remain after the broadcast ends. Excessive
    use may make the phone slow or unstable by causing it to use too
    much memory.

android.permission.SET_WALLPAPER.full_description: Allows the app to set the system wallpaper.

android.permission.READ_PHONE_STATE.full_description:
Allows the app to access the phone
      features of the device.  This permission allows the app to determine the
      phone number and device IDs, whether a call is active, and the remote number
      connected by a call.

android.permission.VIBRATE.full_description: Allows the app to control the vibrator.

android.permission.ACCESS_WIFI_STATE.full_description:
Allows the app to view information
      about Wi-Fi networking, such as whether Wi-Fi is enabled and name of
      connected Wi-Fi devices.

android.permission.WAKE_LOCK.full_description: Allows the app to prevent the phone from going to sleep.

android.permission.CHANGE_WIFI_STATE.full_description:
Allows the app to connect to and
      disconnect from Wi-Fi access points and to make changes to device
      configuration for Wi-Fi networks.

android.permission.MOUNT_UNMOUNT_FILESYS.full_description:
Allows the app to mount and
        unmount filesystems for removable storage.

android.permission.RESTART_PACKAGES.full_description:
Allows the app to end
      background processes of other apps.  This may cause other apps to stop
      running.

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature identifies Adobe Extensible Metadata Platform (XMP) identifiers embedded within files. Defined as a standard for mapping graphical asset relationships, XMP allows for tracking of both parent-child relationships and individual revisions. There are three categories of identifiers: original document, document, and instance. Generally, XMP data is stored in XML format, updated on save/copy, and embedded within the graphical asset. These identifiers can be used to track both malicious and benign graphics within common Microsoft and Adobe document lures.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs
